pitfall: BatchNorm is for CNNs/ResNets. Transformers (BERT, GPT) use LayerNorm. Adding BatchNorm to a Transformer is structurally incompatible.
Decoder-only models (GPT-2) need a classification head for classification tasks. Use AutoModelForSequenceClassification, not AutoModelForCausalLM.
Knowledge distillation requires teacher to be significantly more capable than student. Same-size or same-architecture models make distillation pointless.

Attention mechanism differences (linear vs softmax) may only show meaningful impact at scale (>100M params). On small models, differences may be noise — but a null result ('no significant difference at small scale') is still valid research if framed as a hypothesis test.
MNIST is too simple for meaningful comparisons — any modern method achieves 97%+. Use CIFAR-10 minimum, or CIFAR-100/ImageNet subset for more discriminative results.
Optimizer comparisons need tasks with complex loss landscapes. Simple tasks (MNIST, small MLP) converge regardless of optimizer. Use at least CIFAR-10 with ResNet or a text classification task.
Regularization comparisons require a model large enough to overfit. If the model cannot overfit, regularization differences are invisible.
Adam vs SGD is well-established since 2014. Only interesting with specific hypotheses: generalization gap, specific schedules, or on specific task types. State what you expect and why.
BERT outperforms GPT-2 on classification (bidirectional vs unidirectional). This is known. Only interesting with a twist: few-shot, domain adaptation, or parameter-efficient methods.
Comparing different dropout rates is hyperparameter tuning, not research. Needs a specific hypothesis about the mechanism.
Every comparison needs: (1) a hypothesis about expected outcome and why, (2) a strong baseline (not just a weak one), (3) controlling for compute/parameter budget.
Goals must specify: what models, what dataset, what metric, what comparison. 'Study X' is too vague.
For NLP tasks: SST-2 (binary) is good for quick comparisons. IMDB has longer texts (watch truncation). AG News (4-class) is better for showing model capacity differences.
LoRA target modules differ by architecture: BERT uses query/key/value, GPT-2 uses c_attn/c_proj. Wrong targets = no learning. Also: PEFT on MPS often crashes — use CPU.
